name: Saffron Bread (Wedman)
recipeInstructions:
1. Steep the saffron in the hot water (120 to 130 degrees) for 10 to 15 minutes.
2. In a saucepan, combine the milk and margarine. Heat until the margarine has melted. Cool.
3. Add the lemon rind. Sprinkle the yeast into the lukewarm milk mixture. Let stand for 5 minutes to dissolve.
4. Add the sugar, nutmeg, 2 cups of the flour, and the saffron mixture. Beat until smooth.
5. Stir in the currants (or dark raisins) and another 1/2 cup of the flour. Cover with a damp towel and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size.
6. Punch down and knead on a lightly-floured board until smooth. Shape into a loaf and place in an oiled 4- by 9-inch bread pan.
7. Let rise until doubled. Bake in a 350-degree oven for about 1 hour. Cool for 10 minutes in the pan before turning out on a wire rack. When thoroughly cooled, slice.
Serves 15
One Serving: Calories: 160; Carbohydrates: 20; Protein: 3; Fat: 3; Sodium: 46; Potassium: 132; Cholesterol: 0
Exchange Value: 1 Bread Exchange + 1/2 Fruit Exchange + 1/2 Fat Exchange
